Public Enemy No. 1.description: Public Enemy No. 1 (PEN1) is a street gang with prison presence formed in Long Beach, California, in the 1980s. Based in Orange County, it engages in identity theft, credit card fraud, and methamphetamine trafficking. The gang has ties to the Aryan Brotherhood and Nazi Lowriders. As of 2004, it had around 200 members statewide, with an estimated 400-500 members currently.

Taylor Street Jousters.description: The Taylor Street Jousters were a Chicago street gang originating in Little Italy, Chicago, in the 1950s or 1960s. They were primarily Italian and Mexican, known for their medieval knight symbols. The gang was allied with the Chicago Outfit and Gaylords, and rivaled the Gangster Disciples, Latin Kings, and Simon City Royals. They were active until 2005.

Satudarah.description: Satudarah is a one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1990 in Moordrecht, Netherlands, by nine Moluccan friends. The club has been involved in violent crime, extortion, drug trafficking, and arms trafficking. It was banned in the Netherlands in 2018 and has chapters in numerous countries worldwide.

Lazarus Group.description: State-sponsored hacker group alleged to be run by the North Korean government. Known for cyberattacks since the 2000s, including the 2014 Sony Pictures hack and the 2016 Bangladesh Bank heist. Designated as an advanced persistent threat. Operates globally for espionage and financial theft.

Islamic State – Khorasan Province.description: Regional branch of the Islamic State active in Central and South Asia, primarily Afghanistan and Pakistan. Founded on 26 January 2015 by former Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan members. Seeks to establish a caliphate in the historic Khorasan region. Responsible for numerous terror attacks, including the 2021 Kabul airport attack and the 2024 Crocus City Hall attack in Russia. Currently led by Shahab al-Muhajir.

Los Huistas.description: Los Huistas are a powerful Guatemalan criminal organization based in Huehuetenango, near the Mexican border. Founded in the late 1990s, they control a strategic drug trafficking route for cocaine, methamphetamine, and heroin from Central America to the United States. They have evolved from transporters to producers of methamphetamine and money launderers, infiltrating state institutions. The group maintains a centralized hierarchy and is led by Eugenio Darío Molina-López until his capture in 2026.

descriptions: Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala was formed to oppose Los Huistas' dominance.
edges.evidence_quote: En 2024-2025, surgieron tensiones cuando Los Huistas comenzaron a cobrar 'impuestos' por el paso de drogas a otros grupos locales, como la banda de 'Tío Balde'. Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala, buscando hacer frente a la hegemonía de Los Huistas.

descriptions: Los Huistas act as a logistical link for CJNG, transporting drugs.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo controla una ruta estratégica crítica para el tránsito de cocaína, metanfetaminas y heroína desde Centroamérica hacia Estados Unidos, actuando como un eslabón logístico fundamental para los grandes carteles mexicanos, como el Cartel de Sinaloa y el Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG).

descriptions: Los Huistas initially worked as local allies for the Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Inicialmente, las operaciones en la región estaban controladas por traficantes mexicanos independientes y carteles como Sinaloa y del Golfo, quienes necesitaban aliados locales con experiencia en contrabando y conocimiento del terreno montañoso

descriptions: Los Huistas act as a logistical link for Sinaloa, transporting drugs.
edges.evidence_quote: El grupo controla una ruta estratégica crítica para el tránsito de cocaína, metanfetaminas y heroína desde Centroamérica hacia Estados Unidos, actuando como un eslabón logístico fundamental para los grandes carteles mexicanos, como el Cartel de Sinaloa y el Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG).

descriptions: Los Huistas imposed taxes on Tío Balde, causing tensions.

Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips.description: The Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips are a set of the Crips street gang alliance, formed by Belizean American Crips who moved from South Los Angeles to Belize and then to Harlem, New York. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, prostitution, murder, and robbery. It was established in New York City in the late 1980s or early 1990s by Dalmin "Diamond" Mayen and his associates. The gang remains active in Los Angeles, New York City, and Belize City.

descriptions: Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips are rivals of the Bloods, as indicated by the assault on a girl wearing Bloods colors.
edges.evidence_quote: In November 1995 after several members assaulted a teenaged girl who was wearing red clothing — the gang colors of the rival Bloods gang.

descriptions: Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips is a subset of the Crips alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips are a " set " of the Crips alliance of street gangs.

Clan Mazzarella.description: The Clan Mazzarella is a Camorra criminal organization operating in several neighborhoods of Naples, Italy. Historically considered one of the most powerful Camorra clans, it was founded by the Zaza family in the 1940s and later came under the control of the Mazzarella brothers. Currently, it is divided into several branches, each with its own regent.

edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

'Ndrina Pino.description: The 'Ndrina Pino is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. It was historically allied with the Sena family and later merged into the Perna clan after a feud in the 1980s. The clan was led by Franco Pino, who became a pentito in 1995. It engaged in drug trafficking, arms possession, and international criminal activities, including involvement in the construction of Bucharest airport in Romania.

Tren de Aragua.description: Transnational criminal organization originating from Venezuela, formed around 2007 in Aragua state. Initially involved in extortion linked to a railway construction union, it consolidated power from 2013 at the Tocorón prison under Héctor 'Niño' Guerrero. Engages in drug trafficking, human trafficking, extortion, illegal mining, and money laundering across Latin America, the US, and Spain. Designated as a terrorist group by several countries.

The Brotherhood of Eternal Love.description: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love was an organization of drug users and distributors operating from the mid-1960s through the late 1970s in Orange County, California. Founded by John Griggs, it produced and distributed LSD and hashish with the goal of sparking a 'psychedelic revolution' in the United States. The group was known for the 'Orange Sunshine' LSD tablets and assisted Timothy Leary's escape from prison. Its activities largely ended after a 1972 drug raid, though some members were arrested as late as 2009.

descriptions: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love was formed by members of the Street Sweepers gang, who reincorporated as the new organization.
edges.evidence_quote: In the mid-1960s, John Griggs and others were members of a car gang called the Street Sweepers in Anaheim, California. ... he and the other Street Sweepers became psychedelic evangelists and reincorporated themselves as the Brotherhood of Eternal Love.

descriptions: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love hired the Weather Underground to assist Timothy Leary's escape.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1970 The Brotherhood of Eternal Love hired the radical left organization Weather Underground for a fee of $25,000 to help Timothy Leary make his way to Algeria after he escaped from prison.

descriptions: Weather Underground accepted payment from The Brotherhood of Eternal Love to break Timothy Leary out of prison.
edges.evidence_quote: the group accepted a $25,000 payment from the largest international psychedelic drug distribution organization, called The Brotherhood of Eternal Love, to break LSD advocate Timothy Leary out of a California prison

descriptions: The Celtic Club (Greene) backed John Nardi's faction against the Licavoli faction of Mayfield Road Mob.

descriptions: Mayfield Road Mob employed Danny Greene, leader of The Celtic Club, as muscle.

Ang Soon Tong.description: Ang Soon Tong is a secret society and gang based in Singapore and Malaysia, active since the 1950s. It operates mainly in the Sembawang area of Singapore and engages in criminal activities such as drug trafficking, arms smuggling, illegal moneylending, and illegal gambling. The gang has been involved in violent clashes and murders, with several members sentenced to death or imprisonment.
